questions.text: Which statement best describes analogue data?

options.text: Data stored only as 0s and 1s
Continuous data represented in a physical way
Data that can only be stored in the cloud
Compressed data with no quality loss

questions.explanation: Analogue data is continuous and may be represented physically, such as sound waves, light or grooves in a record.

questions.text: Which statement best describes digital data?

options.text: Data stored using continuous physical values
Data that cannot be edited
Data represented using discrete binary values
Data stored only on optical media

questions.explanation: Digital data is represented using discrete values, usually binary digits: 0s and 1s.

questions.text: Why is analogue data converted to digital data?

options.text: So it can be processed and stored by digital devices
So it becomes impossible to copy
So it no longer needs storage space
So it always becomes higher quality

questions.explanation: Computers and digital devices process and store binary data, so analogue signals must be converted before they can be used digitally.

options.text: The number of pixels in an image
The number of samples taken per second
The number of files stored on a device
The number of colours in a bitmap

questions.explanation: Sample rate is the number of samples captured per second when converting analogue sound into digital data.

questions.text: What is one likely result of increasing both sample rate and sample resolution?

options.text: Lower sound quality and smaller file size
Higher sound quality and larger file size
No change in quality or file size
The sound becomes analogue again

questions.explanation: Higher sample rate and resolution usually improve quality, but more data must be stored, so the file size increases.

options.text: A complete video file
A type of optical storage
A small individual dot that makes up a digital image
A mathematical formula used only in vector graphics

questions.explanation: A bitmap image is made up of many tiny dots called pixels.

questions.text: Which statement correctly compares bitmap and vector graphics?

options.text: Bitmap graphics use pixels, while vector graphics use mathematical shapes
Vector graphics always lose quality when enlarged
Bitmap graphics are always smaller than vector graphics
Bitmap graphics cannot contain colour

questions.explanation: Bitmap graphics are made from pixels, while vector graphics use mathematical descriptions such as points, lines and shapes.

questions.text: What is the main difference between lossy and lossless compression?

options.text: Lossy compression keeps all data; lossless removes data permanently
Lossy compression can reduce quality; lossless compression preserves quality
Lossless compression always makes files larger than the original
Lossy compression can only be used on text files

questions.explanation: Lossy compression removes some data and may reduce quality. Lossless compression allows the original data to be restored without quality loss.

questions.text: How many bits are in one byte?
Which storage medium stores data remotely using the Internet?

options.text: Magnetic storage
Optical storage
Cloud storage
Solid state storage only

questions.explanation: Cloud storage stores data remotely on servers accessed through the Internet.
